2606.15601.title: SCAN: A Decision-Making Framework for Effective Task Allocation with Generative AI
2606.15601.paper_summary: We introduce SCAN -- a human-centric decision-making framework to facilitate learners for effective task allocation with Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) based on Vygotsky's Zone of Proximal Development and Metacognition. In SCAN, we systematize and formalize AI-human interaction by introducing a task-identification approach with four "sub-zones": Substitute, Complement, Aid, and Non-negotiable. After describing the four sub-zones, we demonstrate how SCAN framework can be applied for knowledge workers in the workplace and students in education to metacognitively "scan" their use of Generative AI. We then discuss how such framework can be related to cognitive load theory, cognitive offloading, sycophancy, three decision-making modes in human-AI interactions (automation, augmentation, and collaboration), future of work such as upskilling and deskilling, and how it accounts for both human-human and human-AI learning. We propose that SCAN offers a great starting point before discussing whether GenAI complements or replaces our abilities when completing a task, with a general objective of sustaining lifelong learning, and a specific goal of reaching hybrid intelligence.
2606.15601.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹了SCAN——一個以人為中心的決策框架，旨在幫助學習者基於維果茨基的最近發展區和元認知，利用生成性人工智慧（GenAI）進行有效的任務分配。在SCAN中，我們通過引入一種任務識別方法，系統化並正式化了AI與人類的互動，該方法包含四個“子區域”：替代、補充、輔助和不可協商。描述完這四個子區域後，我們展示了SCAN框架如何應用於職場的知識工作者和教育中的學生，以元認知的方式“掃描”他們對生成性AI的使用。我們接著討論了這種框架如何與認知負荷理論、認知卸載、拍馬屁、人類與AI互動中的三種決策模式（自動化、增強和合作）、未來工作（如技能提升和技能下降）相關，以及它如何考慮人類與人類及人類與AI的學習。我們提出，SCAN提供了一個很好的起點，在討論GenAI是否補充或取代我們完成任務的能力之前，其一般目標是支持終身學習，具體目標是達到混合智能。
2606.15601.author: Fendi Tsim et.al.
